title: Teacher Forcing in Seq2Seq
concept: During training, teacher forcing feeds the correct target word from the dataset to the decoder's next step, keeping training stable.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of a Seq2Seq model?

options.text: To translate or convert one sequence into another.
To delete random words from a document.
To calculate the math equations in a spreadsheet.

exercises.explain: A Seq2Seq (Sequence-to-Sequence) model is designed to turn one sequence (like an English sentence) into another (like a Spanish sentence).

cards.title: Lost in Translation
cards.body: When a model is just starting to learn how to translate sentences, it makes a lot of mistakes. If it gets the first word wrong, its next guesses will get worse and worse, like a runaway train. How do we keep it on track? We use a guide!

cards.title: What is Teacher Forcing?
cards.body: Teacher Forcing is a training trick for Seq2Seq models. Instead of feeding the model's own (often incorrect) guess into the next step, we feed it the actual, correct word from our dataset. This keeps the model from getting hopelessly confused early on.

cards.title: The Piano Teacher
cards.body: Imagine you are teaching a child to play a song on the piano. If they hit a wrong note, instead of letting them struggle and mess up the whole song, you immediately point to the correct next key. This keeps them on the right path and speeds up learning. That's exactly how Teacher Forcing guides a model during its practice runs!

cards.title: Teacher Forcing in Action
cards.body:
Imagine training a model to translate "Hello" to "Hola". 

At step 1, the model accidentally guesses "Heli" instead of "Ho". 

Without Teacher Forcing: We feed "Heli" into step 2, and the model gets even more confused.
With Teacher Forcing: We ignore "Heli" and feed the correct word "Ho" into step 2, keeping the lesson on track!

cards.title: Myth: Always Active
cards.body: Some people think Teacher Forcing is used when the model is actually deployed in the real world. In reality, it is only used during training! When the model is tested or used by real users, it must rely on its own previous predictions.

cards.title: Side-by-Side Comparison
cards.body: Compare how the model learns during training with and without the help of Teacher Forcing.

right.text: An early mistake feeds into the next step, leading to a spiral of bad predictions.

left.text: The model gets the true correct word at each step, keeping training fast and stable.

exercises.prompt: What is the main problem of training WITHOUT Teacher Forcing?

options.text: An early mistake can cause all future guesses to be wrong.
The model forgets the input sentence completely.

exercises.explain: Without Teacher Forcing, one early mistake can ruin the entire sequence because the model keeps feeding its own bad guesses into the next steps.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Teacher forcing is used when the model is generating translations for real users after training is done.
exercises.explain: Teacher forcing is a training-only helper. During testing, the model does not have access to the correct answers, so it cannot use teacher forcing.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete how we keep the decoder on track.
exercises.sentence: Teacher forcing feeds the correct [blank] word to the decoder's next step.
exercises.explain: By feeding the actual target word from our training data, we prevent the model from drifting off into total gibberish when it makes an early mistake.

exercises.prompt: Spot the system that is using Teacher Forcing during training.

options.text: System A: It gets the correct ground-truth word at every step.
System B: It gets its own previous prediction at every step.
System C: It gets the original input sentence in reverse.

exercises.explain: System A is using Teacher Forcing (always receiving the correct ground truth). System B is not using it (relying on its own previous output). System C is a standard encoder action.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is true about Teacher Forcing?

options.text: It makes the model train much slower.
It is only used during training, not during testing.
It forces the human teacher to write the code.
It prevents the model from ever learning.

exercises.explain: Teacher forcing is only used during training to keep the model on track. During actual testing or generation, the model must rely on its own previous guesses, otherwise it wouldn't know what to do in the real world!
